How to Start a Clothing Brand and Find Your Manufacturer

July 1, 2026 | By FABRIOZA

Starting a clothing brand is one of the most rewarding entrepreneurial journeys — and one of the most challenging. After helping 500+ brands launch, we've distilled the process into a clear 7-step roadmap. Follow this guide and you'll go from idea to selling your first garment faster than you think.

Step 1: Define Your Brand Identity

Before designing a single garment, answer these questions:

  • Who is your customer? (Age, lifestyle, income, values)
  • What problem do you solve? (Fit, price, style, sustainability?)
  • What is your price point? (Budget, mid-range, premium, luxury?)
  • What makes you different? (Your unique value proposition)

Example: "We make premium heavyweight streetwear for men aged 20-35 who value quality over fast fashion."

Step 2: Design Your First Collection

Start small. One hero product is better than five mediocre ones.

  • Create mood boards on Pinterest
  • Sketch your designs (even rough sketches work)
  • Reference existing garments you love
  • Specify fabrics, colors, and construction details

Pro tip: Your first collection should have 1-3 pieces maximum. Perfect those before expanding.

Step 3: Find Your Manufacturer (This is Where FABRIOZA Comes In)

Look for a manufacturer that offers:

  • Low MOQ (50 pieces lets you test without massive investment)
  • Free or low-cost sampling
  • Clear communication in your language
  • Quality certifications (ISO 9001)
  • Transparent pricing with no hidden fees
  • Photo documentation during production

Read our complete guide on finding a clothing manufacturer →

Step 4: Develop Your Sample

The sample is everything. It reveals:

  • Whether the manufacturer understood your design
  • Quality of construction and finishing
  • Accuracy of sizing and fit
  • Fabric feel and performance

At FABRIOZA, samples cost $50-80. We always recommend sampling first so you can check quality, fit, and finish before committing to bulk.

Step 5: Plan Your Pricing

Use this simple formula:

Retail Price = (Manufacturing Cost × 2.5 to 4)

Example: If your hoodie costs $15 to manufacture, retail it for $37.50-60. This covers your costs, marketing, shipping to customers, and leaves profit.

Step 6: Place Your First Order

Start with 50-100 pieces. Launch through:

  • Pre-order campaign on Instagram/TikTok
  • Shopify store with organic marketing
  • Pop-up shop or local market
  • Influencer seeding (send free products to 10-20 micro-influencers)

Step 7: Gather Feedback and Iterate

Your first collection is a learning experiment. Collect feedback:

  • What sold out first? (Make more of that)
  • What didn't sell? (Discontinue or redesign)
  • What did customers ask for? (New product ideas)
  • What fit issues came up? (Adjust sizing)

Timeline: From Idea to Launch

PhaseTimeCost
Brand identity & design1-2 weeks$0
Find manufacturer1 week$0
Sample development1-2 weeks$50-80
Sample revisions1 week$0-50
First production (50 pcs)2-3 weeks$400-900
Shipping1 week$80-150
Total to Launch7-12 weeks$530-1,180
